transcript: Apparently, they used to shove bits of raw mutton under their saddles as they rode, tenderizing their dinner. Soon after, the Russians adopted a minced raw beef, added spices, and eventually the dish made its way all the way to Germany 300 years later, where chopped beef was served cooked or raw. Germans emigrating to the United States in the mid-19th century had to leave out of the port of Hamburg. They would sometimes have to wait months for...
